description_long: This is a rectangular barn with a classic gable roof covered in overlapping wooden shingles, giving it a rustic appearance. The front facade features a large, central double door with a diagonal cross-brace design, flanked by two symmetrically placed windows with multiple panes. The exterior walls are clad in a weathered brick pattern, adding to the aged, utilitarian look typical of agricultural outbuildings. The roof extends slightly beyond the walls, providing some overhang and shadow. The rear and side walls are solid brick with no visible openings, emphasizing security and storage functionality. The overall structure is robust and utilitarian, suitable for storage or shelter in a rural or peri-urban setting.
description: A rectangular barn with a gable shingle roof, brick siding, a central double door, and two front windows. The design is rustic and functional, typical of traditional American barns.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double barn door and two symmetrically placed windows on a brick facade beneath a gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full expanse of the shingle-covered gable roof and a solid brick wall with no openings.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the plain brick wall and the overhanging gable roof, with no doors or windows visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the shingle roof and a solid brick wall.
attribute_door_style: single large hinged double door with diagonal cross-brace
attribute_window_arrangement: two windows symmetrically placed on either side of the main door on the front facade
